question: How many hours per week are interns expected to work?
answer: a minimum of 35 hours per week
answer_paragraph: Our intern policy is designed to provide a comprehensive and supportive environment for our interns to learn and grow with our organization. At Smith and Co., we believe that our interns are an integral part of our team and play a crucial role in driving our business forward. Our intern program is open to students and recent graduates from various disciplines, and we receive over 500 applications every year. We have a dedicated team of HR professionals, led by our HR Manager, Emily Chen, who oversee the entire intern program, from recruitment to onboarding and finally to the completion of the internship. The internship period is typically 12 weeks long, and interns are expected to work a minimum of 35 hours per week.

question: What is the stipend paid to interns?

question: Who addresses the interns at the beginning of each internship period?
answer_paragraph: We encourage our interns to participate in company-wide events and meetings, and we also organize regular social events specifically for our interns. For example, last year, we organized a summer picnic for our interns, which was attended by over 50 interns and HR staff. Our CEO, Michael Lee, also addresses our interns at the beginning of each internship period, welcoming them to the company and outlining our expectations.

question: What percentage of interns are offered full-time positions with the company?
answer_paragraph: We are proud to say that many of our interns have gone on to become full-time employees with our company, and we believe that our intern program plays a crucial role in identifying and developing future talent. In fact, according to our HR data, over 70% of our interns are offered full-time positions with the company after completing their internship.

question: How are remote interns expected to participate in company-wide events and meetings?
